mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals imminent labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >60% of instances where the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint decline condition yields positive forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns during early-cycle expansions, or the correlation between INDPRO growth and 20-day returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Positive payroll revisions consistently coincide with negative 20-day forward returns, or the predictive IC remains below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 298, elevated inflation expectations compress real discount rates and trigger central bank tightening fears, producing negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold condition is met but forward 5-day returns are positive in >65% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP and month-over-month increases in retail sales confirm synchronized demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a twenty-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shocks, or forward returns are negative in >50% of synchronized growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index during a credit crunch regime signal severe supply-side constraints and falling business investment, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns during credit crunch periods, or the out-of-sample R-squared for the regime-specific model is below 0.05.
